ProjectName: Dabbler AI Orchestration VS Code extension
Notes: UAT must be executed against a LOCAL build (F5 extension host or packaged .vsix), NOT a published build. This checklist covers the three new Session Set Explorer features from Set 061 and includes a no-regression sweep of the Set 060 Getting Started surfaces.

Review.ItemLabel: N/M+ renders on Lightweight dedicated-sessions set
Review.HumanAction: 1. Open a workspace with a `tier: lightweight`, `verificationMode: dedicated-sessions` set that has completed its work sessions but has no verification session. 2. In the Session Set Explorer, observe the session fraction on that row.
Review.Expectation: The session fraction shows a trailing '+', e.g., '3/3+'. The fraction does not increment the denominator.

Review.ItemLabel: N/M+ fraction has correct tooltip
Review.HumanAction: Hover the mouse over the session fraction from the previous test.
Review.Expectation: A tooltip appears with the exact text: 'Lightweight dedicated-sessions set: verification/remediation sessions are appended when the work sessions complete, so the session count can still grow.'

Review.ItemLabel: N/M+ drops after verification session is added
Review.HumanAction: 1. Using the same set, append a `type: 'verification'` session to the ledger. 2. Reload the view. 3. Observe the session fraction.
Review.Expectation: The '+' disappears, and the fraction becomes an honest N/M, e.g., '3/4'.

Review.ItemLabel: N/M+ is absent on Full-tier and Lightweight Mode-A sets
Review.HumanAction: In the Session Set Explorer, observe a Full-tier set and a `tier: lightweight`, `verificationMode: out-of-band-or-none` set.
Review.Expectation: Neither row shows a '+' on its session fraction, regardless of session state.

Review.ItemLabel: 'lw' marker appears on Lightweight sets
Review.HumanAction: Observe any `tier: lightweight` set row in the Session Set Explorer.
Review.Expectation: A small 'lw' marker appears after the set name. The cursor changes to a help cursor when hovering the marker.

Review.ItemLabel: 'lw' marker is de-emphasized and unobtrusive
Review.HumanAction: Visually inspect the 'lw' marker on a Lightweight set row.
Review.Expectation: The marker is visually quiet, de-emphasized (e.g., lower opacity or muted color), and does not distract from the set name.

Review.ItemLabel: 'lw' marker has correct tooltip
Review.HumanAction: Hover the mouse over the 'lw' marker.
Review.Expectation: A tooltip appears with the exact text: 'Lightweight tier — router-off; verification per the set's verificationMode.'

Review.ItemLabel: 'lw' marker is absent on Full-tier sets
Review.HumanAction: Observe any Full-tier set row in the Session Set Explorer.
Review.Expectation: No 'lw' marker is present.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked marker '⛓︎' appears on unsatisfied non-terminal rows
Review.HumanAction: 1. Open a workspace with a 'not started' or 'in progress' set that has at least one unsatisfied prerequisite. 2. Observe the set row in the Explorer.
Review.Expectation: A chains glyph '⛓︎' appears after the set name.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked marker '⛓︎' renders as a text glyph
Review.HumanAction: Visually inspect the '⛓︎' marker.
Review.Expectation: The marker renders as a standard monochrome text glyph, not a color emoji.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked marker tooltip correctly lists multiple/unknown prerequisites
Review.HumanAction: 1. Use a fixture set that depends on one 'in progress' prerequisite and one prerequisite with a typo'd, unresolvable slug. 2. Hover the '⛓︎' marker.
Review.Expectation: Tooltip appears with the exact format: 'Blocked by prerequisites: <slug1> (in progress), <bad-slug> (unknown set — check the slug) — all must complete first.'

Review.ItemLabel: Old '[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]' description badge is absent
Review.HumanAction: Observe the same blocked set row from the previous test.
Review.Expectation: The description area of the tree item does NOT contain the old all-caps '[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]' text.

Review.ItemLabel: Blocked marker is suppressed on terminal-state rows
Review.HumanAction: 1. Open a workspace where a 'complete' or 'cancelled' set has an unsatisfied prerequisite. 2. Observe the set row.
Review.Expectation: No '⛓︎' marker is visible on the row.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Open Prerequisite Spec' action opens the correct file
Review.HumanAction: 1. Right-click on a blocked set row with an unsatisfied prerequisite. 2. Select 'Open Prerequisite Spec'.
Review.Expectation: The `spec.md` file for the prerequisite set is opened in the editor.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Switch Tier…' appears ONLY on not-started rows
Review.HumanAction: 1. Right-click a 'not-started' set. 2. Right-click an 'in-progress' set. 3. Right-click a 'complete' set. 4. Right-click a 'cancelled' set.
Review.Expectation: The 'Switch Tier…' action is present in the context menu ONLY for the 'not-started' set.

Review.ItemLabel: Switching Lightweight → Full rewrites spec and refreshes
Review.HumanAction: 1. On a 'not-started' Lightweight set, right-click and select 'Switch Tier…'. 2. Choose 'Full' from the QuickPick. 3. Check the set's `spec.md` file and the Explorer view.
Review.Expectation: The `spec.md` is updated to `tier: full`. The file is otherwise byte-identical. The Explorer view refreshes, and the row no longer has the 'lw' marker.

Review.ItemLabel: Switching Full → Lightweight rewrites spec and refreshes
Review.HumanAction: 1. On a 'not-started' Full set, right-click and select 'Switch Tier…'. 2. Choose 'Lightweight' from the QuickPick. 3. Check the set's `spec.md` file and the Explorer view.
Review.Expectation: The `spec.md` is updated to `tier: lightweight`. The file is otherwise byte-identical. The Explorer view refreshes, and the row now has the 'lw' marker.

Review.ItemLabel: Switch-to-Full guardrail fires warning if no provider key is visible
Review.HumanAction: 1. Ensure no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, or `GEMINI_API_KEY` is set in the environment. 2. Switch a set to 'Full' tier.
Review.Expectation: The tier switch succeeds. An info toast confirms the switch, and a separate WARNING toast appears with text like: 'No provider API key (...) is visible to VS Code — Full-tier routing needs at least one...'

Review.ItemLabel: Switch-to-Full guardrail fires warning if router-config is missing
Review.HumanAction: 1. Ensure the workspace root does NOT contain `ai_router/router-config.yaml`. 2. Switch a set to 'Full' tier.
Review.Expectation: The tier switch succeeds. An info toast confirms the switch, and a separate WARNING toast appears with text like: 'ai_router/router-config.yaml was not found in this workspace...'

Review.ItemLabel: Shows 'already on tier' message for same-tier selection
Review.HumanAction: 1. On a 'not-started' Full set, invoke 'Switch Tier…'. 2. Select 'Full' again.
Review.Expectation: An INFO toast appears with text like: '"<name>" is already on the full tier.'

Review.ItemLabel: Repairs malformed tier declaration on same-tier selection
Review.HumanAction: 1. On a not-started set, manually edit the spec.md Session Set Configuration block to a malformed/typo'd tier scalar, e.g. `tier: ful` (NOT a commented `# tier:` line and NOT a valid value with a trailing comment — those are preserved, not repaired). 2. Invoke 'Switch Tier…' and select the canonical tier the value was a typo of (e.g. 'Full').
Review.Expectation: An INFO toast appears with text like: 'Repaired "<name>"'s malformed tier declaration — now explicitly full.' The spec.md tier scalar is corrected to `tier: full` and everything else in the file is byte-identical.

Review.ItemLabel: No-folder CTA view is shown correctly
Review.HumanAction: Open VS Code with no folder/workspace open. View the Dabbler AI side panel.
Review.Expectation: The 'Open Folder' call-to-action view is displayed.

Review.ItemLabel: Getting Started form appears in a workspace with no sets
Review.HumanAction: Open a project folder that has no session sets yet (no docs/session-sets/<set> folders). View the Dabbler AI Orchestration side panel.
Review.Expectation: The Getting Started form is displayed — a Full/Lightweight tier radio, a 'create parallel session sets' checkbox, and the staged action buttons (Build project structure / Import or copy plan / Build session sets). It does NOT prompt for a first-session-set title or name.

Review.ItemLabel: Getting Started form shows Full-tier key/router warnings
Review.HumanAction: 1. In the Getting Started form, select 'Full' tier. 2. Ensure no provider key is set and no router-config exists.
Review.Expectation: The form displays the warnings about needing a provider key and the `ai-router` configuration.

Review.ItemLabel: Creating a set dismisses form and shows Session Set Explorer
Review.HumanAction: Complete the Getting Started form and create a new Session Set.
Review.Expectation: A new set is created on disk. The Getting Started form is replaced by the Session Set Explorer view, listing the new set.
